answer:
HubSpot APIs primarily support OAuth 2.0 and private app access tokens for authentication [1][2]. 

For OAuth, apps must use the Authorization Code grant type [3][4]. To authenticate, include the access token in the Authorization header as a Bearer token [1][2]. To generate or refresh tokens, send a URL-form encoded POST request to the OAuth token endpoint (e.g., /oauth/v3/token) [5][6].

```shell
curl --header "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
https://api.hubapi.com/crm/v3/objects/contacts
```

Private app access tokens are configured in app settings and used identically in the header [1][2]. Service keys are also available for simple, direct REST API requests [7].
